description: Unique identifier for each recorded network session; not used as a model feature (dropped in Step 3.1)
Size of the network packet captured for the session
Transport-layer protocol used by the network session
Total login attempts (successful + failed) made during the session
Total duration of the user/application session
Encryption method used for the session transport — sessions with no/weak encryption are more vulnerable to interception
Threat-intelligence reputation score for the source IP address; higher values indicate a history of association with malicious activity
Number of failed login attempts within the session — elevated counts suggest brute-force/credential-stuffing behaviour
Browser/user-agent used to establish the session — unusual or spoofed values can indicate automated/bot traffic
Flag indicating whether the session occurred outside typical access hours — a common indicator of unauthorized or automated access
Ground-truth intrusion label: 0 = normal, 1 = attack detected
